**Blank** the homeowner’s smart home lighting system is designed to maintain a constant illumination level of 500 lumens in each room by dynamically adjusting LED outputs based on natural light levels.

**Blank** the living room has a large window admitting natural light modeled by the function $ N(t) = 300 + 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) $, where $ t $ is the time in hours after sunrise. To maintain a constant illumination of 500 lumens, the LED output $ L(t) $ must be adjusted as follows:

$$
L(t) = 500 - N(t) = 500 - \left(300 + 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right) = 200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)
$$

This function ensures that the total illumination $ N(t) + L(t) $ remains constant at 500 lumens throughout the day.

**Blank** the total energy consumption of the LEDs over a 24-hour period can be calculated by integrating the power usage over time. Since the LEDs consume power at a rate of 0.1 watts per lumen-hour, the total energy $ E $ in watt-hours is:

$$
E = \int_0^{24} 0.1 \cdot L(t) \, dt = 0.1 \int_0^{24} \left(200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right) \, dt
$$

$$
E = 0.1 \cdot \left[ \int_0^{24} 200 \, dt - \int_0^{24} 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) \, dt \right]
$$

$$
E = 0.1 \cdot \left[ 200 \cdot 24 - 200 \cdot \int_0^{24} \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) \, dt \right]
$$
